rows.evidence_statement: UniProt identifies human SNCA as alpha-synuclein, a neuronal protein with membrane-associated multimeric functional context.
rows.grammar_role: Target identity and biological ensemble context.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_date_or_version: UniProtKB reviewed P37840; last annotation update 2026-06-10; accessed 2026-06-11

rows.evidence_statement: DisProt records alpha-synuclein as intrinsically disordered/unstructured in the free state.
rows.grammar_role: Free-state IDP grammar: disorder is the default, not a missing coordinate model.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_url_or_citation: https://disprot.org/ ; alpha-synuclein / UniProtKB P37840 disorder annotation

rows.evidence_statement: Alpha-synuclein can undergo disorder-to-order transition toward alpha-helical structure when bound to lipid membranes.
rows.grammar_role: Disorder-to-order grammar without selecting a single native fold.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_url_or_citation: Davidson WS et al. Stabilization of alpha-synuclein secondary structure upon binding to synthetic membranes. J Biol Chem. 1998;273:9443-9449.

rows.evidence_statement: SNCA is treated as an intrinsically disordered ensemble/disorder-to-order problem; V36 forbids forcing it into a single compact native-fold grammar.
rows.grammar_role: IDP firewall: ensemble grammar, no single native fold claim.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_url_or_citation: DisProt alpha-synuclein / UniProtKB P37840; Davidson WS et al. J Biol Chem. 1998;273:9443-9449.
